title: Dinosaur Discovery: Prehistoric World
description: A detailed dinosaur scene featuring various dinosaur species in their natural habitat. This coloring page helps students learn about different dinosaurs while developing fine motor skills and creativity.
instructions: Color the dinosaurs and their environment using crayons, colored pencils, or markers. Use the learning facts below to help you choose realistic colors for each dinosaur!

content.image: A prehistoric scene showing five different dinosaurs in their natural habitat. The scene includes a large Tyrannosaurus rex standing tall on the right, a long-necked Brachiosaurus reaching for tree leaves in the background, a horned Triceratops grazing on plants in the foreground, a Pteranodon flying overhead, and a small Velociraptor crouching near some rocks. The background includes volcanoes, prehistoric plants, and a river flowing through the landscape.
learningFacts: Dinosaurs lived during the Mesozoic Era, which lasted from about 252 to 66 million years ago.
Tyrannosaurus rex could grow up to 40 feet long and had teeth as big as bananas!
Brachiosaurus could reach heights of 40-50 feet, making it one of the tallest dinosaurs.
Triceratops used its three horns and large frill to defend itself from predators.
Scientists believe many dinosaurs had colorful skin, and some even had feathers!
Pteranodons weren't dinosaurs but flying reptiles called pterosaurs.
Velociraptors were actually much smaller than shown in movies - about the size of a turkey.
